summary: Controls the global SMB server RejectUnencryptedAccess value under LanmanServer. This is a strict hardening step that refuses clients not using SMB encryption, but it can break legacy clients and mixed environments.

setting.name: SMB Server Reject Unencrypted Access
setting.casual_explanation: This setting makes the SMB server reject clients that try to access shares without encryption. It is stricter than simply preferring safer behavior because noncompliant clients are actively refused.
setting.why_people_change_it: People turn this on when they want a firm encrypted-only rule for SMB access. They leave it off when they still need compatibility with older or less capable clients.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: Rejecting unencrypted access can strengthen security, but any SMB client that cannot negotiate encryption may stop working.
setting.professional_notes: Microsoft documents RejectUnencryptedAccess under HKLM\System\CurrentControlSet\Services\LanmanServer\Parameters as part of SMB encryption behavior. This record documents the app's global registry write only.

allowed_values.label: Windows managed baseline
allowed_values.meaning: Windows keeps its SMB server baseline without an explicit local override for this behavior.

allowed_values.label: Do not reject unencrypted access
allowed_values.meaning: The server does not force this global rejection rule through the registry override.

allowed_values.label: Reject unencrypted access
allowed_values.meaning: The server rejects clients that do not use SMB encryption through this registry override.

app_current_implementation.notes: The current app writes RejectUnencryptedAccess = 1, which matches the documented stricter SMB server behavior.

windows_defaults.label: Windows SMB server baseline on supported releases
windows_defaults.applies_to: Windows 8 and later / Windows Server 2012 and later SMB server releases

states.rationale: Leave the explicit override absent; Microsoft documents TRUE as the default behavior on supported SMB server releases.

windows_defaults.notes: The explicit registry value can stay absent because Microsoft documents RejectUnencryptedAccess as TRUE by default on supported SMB server releases.

recommended_profiles.label: Windows managed default
intended_for: Users who want the normal SMB server baseline
People who are still checking client compatibility
recommended_profiles.good_when: You want Windows or other SMB management surfaces to keep their normal behavior.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You need a firm encrypted-only access policy.
tradeoffs: You avoid forcing a strict rejection rule immediately.
Clients that do not use encryption are not explicitly blocked by this value.

recommended_profiles.label: Encrypted-only access
intended_for: Users who want strict SMB encryption enforcement

states.rationale: Reject clients that do not use SMB encryption.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want a strict rule that blocks SMB clients which do not negotiate encryption.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You still depend on clients that cannot meet this requirement.
tradeoffs: Older or incompatible clients can lose access.

evidence.title: Microsoft SMB security enhancements troubleshooting
evidence.summary: Microsoft documents RejectUnencryptedAccess under HKLM\System\CurrentControlSet\Services\LanmanServer\Parameters as a registry control for SMB encryption behavior.

evidence.title: Current app implementation
evidence.summary: The provider writes RejectUnencryptedAccess = 1 for the current tweak.

decision.why: Microsoft documents the exact registry path, 0/1 behavior, and default TRUE state for supported SMB server releases. The app writes the same documented control surface and the restore story is exact, so this record is app-ready.

validation_proof.exact_quote_or_path: Windows 8 operating system and later and Windows Server 2012 operating system and later by default, set RejectUnencryptedAccess to TRUE. If the registry value RejectUnencryptedAccess under HKLM\System\CurrentControlSet\Services\LanmanServer\Parameters\ is set to zero, RejectUnencryptedAccess is set to FALSE.
validation_proof.notes: Microsoft Open Specifications documents the exact LanmanServer registry path and the default TRUE behavior. The app writes the same documented runtime surface with value 1 to enforce rejection.
